step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the consensus that the most likely diagnosis is an ependymoma. One additional point to support this decision is the rarity of glioblastoma multiforme (option B) in pediatric populations; while it can occur across ages, it is much less common than ependymomas in adolescence. Therefore, the combination of the midline cerebellar location and the patient’s age strongly suggests an ependymoma over glioblastoma multiforme.

Answer Choice: B (Ependymoma). Your agreement includes the added rationale about the relative rarity of glioblastoma multiforme in pediatrics, reinforcing why option B remains the best fit among the provided choices. Final
